content.text:
I have all the information needed. Here is the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Comprehensive Implementation Plan: Per-User and Enterprise-Governed MCP Server Management

### Overview

This plan introduces two-layer MCP server scoping: (1) per-user personal installations via `user_id` on `MCPServerConfig`, and (2) enterprise-level approved/auto-enabled servers via a new `EnterpriseMCPServer` entity. The user's effective server list merges both layers.

---

### Phase 1: OpenAPI Spec Changes (`core/api/openapi.yaml`)

**1A. New `EnterpriseMCPServer` schema** (add in `components/schemas` around line 2554)

```yaml
EnterpriseMCPServer:
  type: object
  required: [name, command]
  properties:
    id:
      type: string
      readOnly: true
    enterprise_id:
      type: string
      readOnly: true
    name:
      type: string
    description:
      type: string
    command:
      type: array
      items: { type: string }
    env:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: { type: string }
    mode:
      type: string
      enum: [local, remote]
      default: local
    version:
      type: string
    registry_id:
      type: string
    auto_enabled:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: If true, this server is automatically active for all enterprise users
    policy_mapping:
      type: object
      properties:
        tool_prefix:
          type: string
    created_at:
      type: string
      format: date-time
      readOnly: true
    updated_at:
      type: string
      format: date-time
      readOnly: true
```

**1B. Add `user_id` and `source` fields to `MCPServerConfig`** (modify schema at line ~2501)

Add two new properties:
- `user_id`: `type: string`, `readOnly: true` -- the owning user
- `source`: `type: string`, `enum: [personal, enterprise]`, `readOnly: true` -- distinguishes origin in merged list responses

**1C. New enterprise MCP server endpoints** (add new path block, e.g., after the marketplace paths around line 1280)

New tag: `Enterprise MCP Servers`

| Method | Path | operationId | Auth |
|--------|------|-------------|------|
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | `listEnterpriseMCPServers` | Any authenticated member |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | `createEnterpriseMCPServer` | owner/admin only |
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | `getEnterpriseMCPServer` | Any authenticated member |
| PUT | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | `updateEnterpriseMCPServer` | owner/admin only |
| DELETE | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | `deleteEnterpriseMCPServer` | owner/admin only |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}/credentials` | `setEnterpriseMCPServerCredential` | owner/admin only |

**1D. Modify existing `listMCPServers` response** to include `source` field enrichment on each item (no schema-level change needed beyond adding `source` to `MCPServerConfig`).

**1E. Modify `MarketplaceServer.installed` description** to note it reflects the user's complete view (personal + enterprise auto-enabled).

---

### Phase 2: Store SPI Changes (`core/store/store.go`)

**2A. Add `UserID` and `EnterpriseID` to `MCPServerFilter`**

Current filter (line 134):
```go
type MCPServerFilter struct {
    Status   *api.MCPServerConfigStatus
    PageSize int
}
```

New:
```go
type MCPServerFilter struct {
    UserID       string
    EnterpriseID string // used for cross-referencing enterprise auto-enabled
    Status       *api.MCPServerConfigStatus
    PageSize     int
}
```

**2B. New `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` interface and filter**

```go
type EnterpriseMCPServerStore interface {
    Create(ctx context.Context, server api.EnterpriseMCPServer) error
    Get(ctx context.Context, serverID string) (api.EnterpriseMCPServer, error)
    List(ctx context.Context, filter EnterpriseMCPServerFilter) ([]api.EnterpriseMCPServer, error)
    Update(ctx context.Context, server api.EnterpriseMCPServer) error
    Delete(ctx context.Context, serverID string) error
}

type EnterpriseMCPServerFilter struct {
    EnterpriseID string
    AutoEnabled  *bool
    PageSize     int
}
```

---

### Phase 3: In-Memory Store Implementations

**3A. Modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`**

The `List` method must filter by `UserID` when set. The map key remains `serverID`, but the filter adds a check:

```go
if filter.UserID != "" && (srv.UserId == nil || *srv.UserId != filter.UserID) {
    continue
}
```

No changes to `Create`, `Get`, `Update`, `Delete` -- they remain ID-keyed. The `user_id` is set by the handler before calling `Create`.

**3B. New file `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go`**

Following the exact pattern of `mcpserver.go`:
- `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` struct with `sync.RWMutex` + `map[string]api.EnterpriseMCPServer`
- `NewEnterpriseMCPServerStore()` constructor
- `Create`, `Get`, `List` (filter by `EnterpriseID`, optionally by `AutoEnabled`), `Update`, `Delete`
- `List` filters: `if filter.EnterpriseID != "" && (srv.EnterpriseId == nil || *srv.EnterpriseId != filter.EnterpriseID) { continue }`
- `if filter.AutoEnabled != nil && (srv.AutoEnabled == nil || *srv.AutoEnabled != *filter.AutoEnabled) { continue }`

---

### Phase 4: Handler Changes (`core/app/handlers.go` and `core/app/handlers_auth.go`)

**4A. Add `enterpriseMCPServers` field to `apiServer` struct** (line 40, alongside `mcpServers`)

```go
enterpriseMCPServers *mem.EnterpriseMCPServerStore
```

**4B. Wire in constructor** -- wherever `NewMCPServerStore()` is called, also call `NewEnterpriseMCPServerStore()`.

**4C. Add auth to ALL existing MCP server handlers** (lines 1155-1469)

Every handler in the user MCP server group needs:
```go
claims := auth.ClaimsFromContext(r.Context())
if claims == nil {
    writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "not authenticated")
    return
}
```

Then scope by `claims.Subject` (the user ID):

- **`ListMCPServers`**: Pass `UserID: claims.Subject` in filter. Then also fetch enterprise auto-enabled servers via `enterpriseMCPServers.List(ctx, store.EnterpriseMCPServerFilter{EnterpriseID: claims.EnterpriseID, AutoEnabled: boolPtr(true)})`. Convert enterprise servers to `MCPServerConfig` shape with `source: "enterprise"`. Mark personal ones with `source: "personal"`. Merge and return.

- **`CreateMCPServer`**: Set `req.UserId = &claims.Subject` before storing. This creates a per-user record.

- **`GetMCPServer`**: After fetching, verify `*srv.UserId == claims.Subject`. If not the user's server, check if it's an enterprise auto-enabled server for their enterprise (to support viewing enterprise servers by their user-facing ID). Return 404 if neither match.

- **`UpdateMCPServer`**: After fetching existing, verify `*existing.UserId == claims.Subject`. Users cannot update enterprise-managed servers. Return 403 if the server is enterprise-sourced.

- **`DeleteMCPServer`**: After checking existence, verify ownership. Users cannot delete enterprise auto-enabled servers. Return 403.

- **`SetMCPServerCredential`**: Verify ownership. Update vault path to include user scoping: `mcp-servers/user/{claims.Subject}/{id}/{env_var}`.

**4D. New enterprise MCP server handlers** (new functions, can go in `handlers_auth.go` or a new `handlers_enterprise_mcp.go`)

All follow the pattern from `UpdateCurrentEnterprise` (line 94 of `handlers_auth.go`):

```go
claims := auth.ClaimsFromContext(r.Context())
if claims == nil {
    writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "not authenticated")
    return
}
```

For write operations, add the admin check:
```go
if claims.Role != string(model.UserRoleOwner) && claims.Role != string(model.UserRoleAdmin) {
    writeError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "forbidden", "owner or admin role required")
    return
}
```

Handlers:

- **`ListEnterpriseMCPServers`**: Any member. Filter by `claims.EnterpriseID`.
- **`CreateEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Admin check. Set `enterprise_id` from claims. Generate `emcp_` prefixed ID. Default `auto_enabled` to false.
- **`GetEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Any member. Verify `enterprise_id` matches claims.
- **`UpdateEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Admin check. Verify enterprise match. Preserve read-only fields.
- **`DeleteEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Admin check. Verify enterprise match.
- **`SetEnterpriseMCPServerCredential`**: Admin check. Vault path: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{claims.EnterpriseID}/{id}/{env_var}`.

**4E. Modify `ListMarketplaceServers`** (line 1307)

The `installed` enrichment (lines 1322-1328) currently lists ALL MCP servers without user scoping. Change to:
1. Fetch user's personal servers with `UserID: claims.Subject`
2. Fetch enterprise auto-enabled servers with `EnterpriseID: claims.EnterpriseID, AutoEnabled: boolPtr(true)`
3. Build `installedSet` from both registry IDs

**4F. Modify `InstallMarketplaceServer`** (line 1401)

Set `config.UserId = &claims.Subject` before creating. This makes marketplace installs per-user.

**4G. Personal enterprise behavior**

No special handling needed. Personal enterprises have `personal: true` but the data model is the same. The sole owner of a personal enterprise has full admin rights, so they can manage their own enterprise approved list. The admin role check (`owner || admin`) naturally allows this.

---

### Phase 5: Code Generation

After modifying `openapi.yaml`, run:
```
task generate:api
```

This runs `oapi-codegen` and regenerates `core/api/gen/server.gen.go`, which will:
- Add the `EnterpriseMCPServer` Go type
- Add `UserId` and `Source` fields to `MCPServerConfig`
- Add new methods to `ServerInterface` for the 6 new endpoints
- Add wrapper functions and route registrations

The new `ServerInterface` methods that `apiServer` must implement:
```go
ListEnterpriseMCPServers(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
CreateEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
GetEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
UpdateEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
DeleteEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
SetEnterpriseMCPServerCredential(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
```

---

### Phase 6: Test Plan

**6A. New file `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver_test.go`**

Following the pattern of `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`:
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_CRUD` -- create, get, list, update, delete
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_NotFound` -- get/update/delete nonexistent
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_ListWithEnterpriseFilter` -- verify enterprise isolation
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_ListWithAutoEnabledFilter` -- verify auto_enabled filtering

**6B. Modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`**

- `TestMCPServerStore_ListWithUserFilter` -- create servers for user_a and user_b, verify list with `UserID: "user_a"` returns only user_a's servers

**6C. Handler integration tests** (new file or extend `core/app/handlers_auth_test.go`)

- Test user A cannot see user B's servers
- Test member cannot create/update/delete enterprise MCP servers (gets 403)
- Test admin can CRUD enterprise MCP servers
- Test `ListMCPServers` returns merged personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- Test enterprise auto-enabled servers appear with `source: "enterprise"` and cannot be deleted by user
- Test marketplace `installed` flag reflects both personal and enterprise servers
- Test `InstallMarketplaceServer` creates per-user record
- Test personal enterprise user can manage their own enterprise approved list

---

### Phase 7: UI Changes

**7A. Modify `ui/src/lib/api.ts`**

Add new API functions:
```typescript
export async function listEnterpriseMCPServers() {
    return apiFetch('/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers');
}
export async function createEnterpriseMCPServer(data: any) {
    return apiFetch('/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) });
}
export async function getEnterpriseMCPServer(id: string) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}`);
}
export async function updateEnterpriseMCPServer(id: string, data: any) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}`, { method: 'PUT', body: JSON.stringify(data) });
}
export async function deleteEnterpriseMCPServer(id: string) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' });
}
export async function setEnterpriseMCPServerCredential(id: string, envVarName: string, secretValue: string) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}/credentials`, {
        method: 'POST',
        body: JSON.stringify({ env_var_name: envVarName, secret_value: secretValue })
    });
}
```

**7B. Modify `ui/src/routes/servers/+page.svelte`**

- Display `source` badge on each server card: "Personal" or "Enterprise" (using the `source` field from the API response).
- For enterprise-sourced servers, hide the "Remove" button (or show it disabled with a tooltip "Managed by your organization").
- The existing `listMCPServers()` call already returns the merged list from the backend, so no additional fetch is needed.

**7C. Modify `ui/src/routes/servers/[id]/+page.svelte`**

- Show `source` indicator in the configuration card.
- If `source === "enterprise"`, hide the "Delete Server" danger zone and the credential-setting form (enterprise credentials are managed by admins).

**7D. New route `ui/src/routes/settings/mcp-servers/+page.svelte`**

Enterprise MCP server management page (accessible from settings layout). This is the admin-facing page for curating the approved list:
- Shows enterprise-level MCP servers with `auto_enabled` toggle
- Add/remove servers (calls enterprise endpoints)
- Set credentials for enterprise servers
- Only visible when `getUser()?.role === 'owner' || getUser()?.role === 'admin'`
- For personal enterprises, this is where the user manages their own approved list

**7E. Modify `ui/src/routes/settings/+layout.svelte`**

Add "MCP Servers" navigation link to settings sidebar, visible for owner/admin roles.

**7F. Modify `ui/src/routes/marketplace/+page.svelte`**

The `installed` flag from the API already covers both personal and enterprise sources (handled backend). No changes needed unless we want to distinguish "installed by you" vs "installed by enterprise" -- which could be a follow-up enhancement.

---

### Implementation Sequence

1. **OpenAPI spec** -- all schema and endpoint changes in `core/api/openapi.yaml`
2. **Code generation** -- `task generate:api` to get new types and interface methods
3. **Store SPI** -- update `MCPServerFilter` and add `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` + filter in `core/store/store.go`
4. **In-memory stores** -- modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go` (UserID filter) and create `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go`
5. **Store tests** -- new and modified tests in `core/store/mem/`
6. **Handlers** -- add auth to existing MCP handlers, add enterprise handlers, modify marketplace handlers
7. **Handler tests** -- integration tests for auth, scoping, and merged list behavior
8. **UI api.ts** -- add enterprise MCP API functions
9. **UI pages** -- modify servers pages, add settings/mcp-servers page

### Potential Challenges

- **Backward compatibility**: Existing MCP servers in the store have no `user_id`. The `ListMCPServers` handler should treat `nil` user_id servers as belonging to the requesting user (or as global/legacy) during migration. A pragmatic approach: if `user_id` is nil on a stored record, include it in any user's list. This handles the dev/test scenario where servers were created before auth was wired in.

- **Auth disabled mode**: The existing handlers don't check auth at all. When `claims` is nil (auth middleware skipped), the handlers should fall back to unscoped behavior. Check `if claims != nil { filter.UserID = claims.Subject }` -- when nil, return all servers (backward compatible with local dev).

- **ID prefix collision**: Enterprise MCP servers should use `emcp_` prefix to distinguish from user MCP servers (`mcp_`). This prevents ID collision and makes it clear from the ID alone which store to query.

- **Vault path migration**: Existing vault paths are `mcp-servers/{id}/{env_var}`. New paths will be `mcp-servers/user/{user_id}/{id}/{env_var}` for user servers and `mcp-servers/enterprise/{enterprise_id}/{id}/{env_var}` for enterprise servers. Existing paths should continue to work for legacy servers.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/openapi.yaml`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/store.go`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app/handlers.go`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app/handlers_auth.go`

toolUseResult.prompt:
Design a comprehensive implementation plan for per-user AND enterprise-governed MCP server management in Aileron. Here is the full context:

## Requirements

1. **Per-user scoping**: When user A installs an MCP server, user B doesn't see it
2. **Enterprise approved list**: Enterprise admins curate a list of MCP servers approved for their enterprise
3. **Enterprise auto-enabled**: Admins can auto-enable servers, making them mandatory for all enterprise users (users cannot remove these)
4. **Enterprise admin controls**: Only owner/admin roles can manage enterprise-level MCP settings
5. **Personal accounts**: Gmail etc. users have `personal: true` enterprises where they're the sole owner — they control their own approved list

## Existing Architecture

### Auth & Roles
- JWT Claims: `Subject` (userID), `EnterpriseID`, `Email`, `Role` (owner|admin|member)
- `auth.ClaimsFromContext(r.Context())` extracts claims
- Admin check pattern: `claims.Role != "owner" && claims.Role != "admin"` → 403
- Personal enterprises have `personal: true`

### Current MCP Server Model
- `MCPServerConfig` schema: id, name, description, command, env, mode, status, version, registry_id, policy_mapping, created_at, updated_at
- No user_id or enterprise_id fields
- Store: `MCPServerStore` with Create/Get/List/Update/Delete, `MCPServerFilter{Status, PageSize}`
- In-memory impl only (no postgres yet)
- Handlers don't check auth at all

### Existing Scoping Patterns
- Business entities (intents, policies) use `WorkspaceID` in filters
- Users scoped by `EnterpriseID` in UserFilter
- Admin-only endpoints check role inline (no helper function)

### Files
- OpenAPI spec: `core/api/openapi.yaml`
- Store SPI: `core/store/store.go`
- In-mem store: `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`
- Handlers: `core/app/handlers.go` (lines 1155-1469)
- Handler tests: `core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`
- Auth: `core/auth/jwt.go`, `core/auth/middleware.go`
- Models: `core/model/enterprise.go`

## Design Considerations

### Data Model
We need TWO layers:
1. **User-level**: `MCPServerConfig` gains `user_id` — the user's personal installations
2. **Enterprise-level**: New `EnterpriseMCPServer` entity — the enterprise's approved/auto-enabled list

### Enterprise MCP Server entity should include:
- `id`, `enterprise_id` (readOnly)
- Same server config fields (name, description, command, env, mode, registry_id, version)
- `auto_enabled` boolean — if true, all enterprise users automatically have this server active
- `created_at`, `updated_at`

### API Design
New endpoints for enterprise MCP management (admin only for writes, members can read):
- `GET /v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` — list enterprise's approved servers
- `POST /v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` — add to approved list (admin only)
- `GET /v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` — get enterprise server details
- `PUT /v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` — update (admin only)
- `DELETE /v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` — remove (admin only)

### User's Effective Server List
When a user calls `GET /v1/mcp-servers`, they should see:
- Their own installed servers (user_id matches)
- Plus all enterprise auto-enabled servers (from their enterprise)
- The response should distinguish between "personal" and "enterprise-managed" servers (maybe a `source` field?)

### Marketplace Behavior
- `installed` flag on marketplace listings should reflect the user's complete view (personal + enterprise auto-enabled)
- When installing from marketplace, creates a per-user record

### Vault paths for enterprise servers
Enterprise server credentials: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{enterprise_id}/{server_id}/{env_var}`
User server credentials: `mcp-servers/user/{user_id}/{server_id}/{env_var}` (or keep existing `mcp-servers/{server_id}/{env_var}`)

Please design a detailed, ordered implementation plan with:
1. OpenAPI spec changes (new schemas, new endpoints, modified endpoints)
2. Store SPI changes (new interface + modified interface)
3. In-memory store implementations
4. Handler changes (new handlers + modified handlers)
5. Test plan
6. UI changes needed

For each step, be specific about what fields, endpoints, and logic changes are needed.

content.text:
I have all the information needed. Here is the comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Comprehensive Implementation Plan: Per-User and Enterprise-Governed MCP Server Management

### Overview

This plan introduces two-layer MCP server scoping: (1) per-user personal installations via `user_id` on `MCPServerConfig`, and (2) enterprise-level approved/auto-enabled servers via a new `EnterpriseMCPServer` entity. The user's effective server list merges both layers.

---

### Phase 1: OpenAPI Spec Changes (`core/api/openapi.yaml`)

**1A. New `EnterpriseMCPServer` schema** (add in `components/schemas` around line 2554)

```yaml
EnterpriseMCPServer:
  type: object
  required: [name, command]
  properties:
    id:
      type: string
      readOnly: true
    enterprise_id:
      type: string
      readOnly: true
    name:
      type: string
    description:
      type: string
    command:
      type: array
      items: { type: string }
    env:
      type: object
      additionalProperties: { type: string }
    mode:
      type: string
      enum: [local, remote]
      default: local
    version:
      type: string
    registry_id:
      type: string
    auto_enabled:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: If true, this server is automatically active for all enterprise users
    policy_mapping:
      type: object
      properties:
        tool_prefix:
          type: string
    created_at:
      type: string
      format: date-time
      readOnly: true
    updated_at:
      type: string
      format: date-time
      readOnly: true
```

**1B. Add `user_id` and `source` fields to `MCPServerConfig`** (modify schema at line ~2501)

Add two new properties:
- `user_id`: `type: string`, `readOnly: true` -- the owning user
- `source`: `type: string`, `enum: [personal, enterprise]`, `readOnly: true` -- distinguishes origin in merged list responses

**1C. New enterprise MCP server endpoints** (add new path block, e.g., after the marketplace paths around line 1280)

New tag: `Enterprise MCP Servers`

| Method | Path | operationId | Auth |
|--------|------|-------------|------|
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | `listEnterpriseMCPServers` | Any authenticated member |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | `createEnterpriseMCPServer` | owner/admin only |
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | `getEnterpriseMCPServer` | Any authenticated member |
| PUT | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | `updateEnterpriseMCPServer` | owner/admin only |
| DELETE | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | `deleteEnterpriseMCPServer` | owner/admin only |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}/credentials` | `setEnterpriseMCPServerCredential` | owner/admin only |

**1D. Modify existing `listMCPServers` response** to include `source` field enrichment on each item (no schema-level change needed beyond adding `source` to `MCPServerConfig`).

**1E. Modify `MarketplaceServer.installed` description** to note it reflects the user's complete view (personal + enterprise auto-enabled).

---

### Phase 2: Store SPI Changes (`core/store/store.go`)

**2A. Add `UserID` and `EnterpriseID` to `MCPServerFilter`**

Current filter (line 134):
```go
type MCPServerFilter struct {
    Status   *api.MCPServerConfigStatus
    PageSize int
}
```

New:
```go
type MCPServerFilter struct {
    UserID       string
    EnterpriseID string // used for cross-referencing enterprise auto-enabled
    Status       *api.MCPServerConfigStatus
    PageSize     int
}
```

**2B. New `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` interface and filter**

```go
type EnterpriseMCPServerStore interface {
    Create(ctx context.Context, server api.EnterpriseMCPServer) error
    Get(ctx context.Context, serverID string) (api.EnterpriseMCPServer, error)
    List(ctx context.Context, filter EnterpriseMCPServerFilter) ([]api.EnterpriseMCPServer, error)
    Update(ctx context.Context, server api.EnterpriseMCPServer) error
    Delete(ctx context.Context, serverID string) error
}

type EnterpriseMCPServerFilter struct {
    EnterpriseID string
    AutoEnabled  *bool
    PageSize     int
}
```

---

### Phase 3: In-Memory Store Implementations

**3A. Modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`**

The `List` method must filter by `UserID` when set. The map key remains `serverID`, but the filter adds a check:

```go
if filter.UserID != "" && (srv.UserId == nil || *srv.UserId != filter.UserID) {
    continue
}
```

No changes to `Create`, `Get`, `Update`, `Delete` -- they remain ID-keyed. The `user_id` is set by the handler before calling `Create`.

**3B. New file `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go`**

Following the exact pattern of `mcpserver.go`:
- `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` struct with `sync.RWMutex` + `map[string]api.EnterpriseMCPServer`
- `NewEnterpriseMCPServerStore()` constructor
- `Create`, `Get`, `List` (filter by `EnterpriseID`, optionally by `AutoEnabled`), `Update`, `Delete`
- `List` filters: `if filter.EnterpriseID != "" && (srv.EnterpriseId == nil || *srv.EnterpriseId != filter.EnterpriseID) { continue }`
- `if filter.AutoEnabled != nil && (srv.AutoEnabled == nil || *srv.AutoEnabled != *filter.AutoEnabled) { continue }`

---

### Phase 4: Handler Changes (`core/app/handlers.go` and `core/app/handlers_auth.go`)

**4A. Add `enterpriseMCPServers` field to `apiServer` struct** (line 40, alongside `mcpServers`)

```go
enterpriseMCPServers *mem.EnterpriseMCPServerStore
```

**4B. Wire in constructor** -- wherever `NewMCPServerStore()` is called, also call `NewEnterpriseMCPServerStore()`.

**4C. Add auth to ALL existing MCP server handlers** (lines 1155-1469)

Every handler in the user MCP server group needs:
```go
claims := auth.ClaimsFromContext(r.Context())
if claims == nil {
    writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "not authenticated")
    return
}
```

Then scope by `claims.Subject` (the user ID):

- **`ListMCPServers`**: Pass `UserID: claims.Subject` in filter. Then also fetch enterprise auto-enabled servers via `enterpriseMCPServers.List(ctx, store.EnterpriseMCPServerFilter{EnterpriseID: claims.EnterpriseID, AutoEnabled: boolPtr(true)})`. Convert enterprise servers to `MCPServerConfig` shape with `source: "enterprise"`. Mark personal ones with `source: "personal"`. Merge and return.

- **`CreateMCPServer`**: Set `req.UserId = &claims.Subject` before storing. This creates a per-user record.

- **`GetMCPServer`**: After fetching, verify `*srv.UserId == claims.Subject`. If not the user's server, check if it's an enterprise auto-enabled server for their enterprise (to support viewing enterprise servers by their user-facing ID). Return 404 if neither match.

- **`UpdateMCPServer`**: After fetching existing, verify `*existing.UserId == claims.Subject`. Users cannot update enterprise-managed servers. Return 403 if the server is enterprise-sourced.

- **`DeleteMCPServer`**: After checking existence, verify ownership. Users cannot delete enterprise auto-enabled servers. Return 403.

- **`SetMCPServerCredential`**: Verify ownership. Update vault path to include user scoping: `mcp-servers/user/{claims.Subject}/{id}/{env_var}`.

**4D. New enterprise MCP server handlers** (new functions, can go in `handlers_auth.go` or a new `handlers_enterprise_mcp.go`)

All follow the pattern from `UpdateCurrentEnterprise` (line 94 of `handlers_auth.go`):

```go
claims := auth.ClaimsFromContext(r.Context())
if claims == nil {
    writeError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "not authenticated")
    return
}
```

For write operations, add the admin check:
```go
if claims.Role != string(model.UserRoleOwner) && claims.Role != string(model.UserRoleAdmin) {
    writeError(w, http.StatusForbidden, "forbidden", "owner or admin role required")
    return
}
```

Handlers:

- **`ListEnterpriseMCPServers`**: Any member. Filter by `claims.EnterpriseID`.
- **`CreateEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Admin check. Set `enterprise_id` from claims. Generate `emcp_` prefixed ID. Default `auto_enabled` to false.
- **`GetEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Any member. Verify `enterprise_id` matches claims.
- **`UpdateEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Admin check. Verify enterprise match. Preserve read-only fields.
- **`DeleteEnterpriseMCPServer`**: Admin check. Verify enterprise match.
- **`SetEnterpriseMCPServerCredential`**: Admin check. Vault path: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{claims.EnterpriseID}/{id}/{env_var}`.

**4E. Modify `ListMarketplaceServers`** (line 1307)

The `installed` enrichment (lines 1322-1328) currently lists ALL MCP servers without user scoping. Change to:
1. Fetch user's personal servers with `UserID: claims.Subject`
2. Fetch enterprise auto-enabled servers with `EnterpriseID: claims.EnterpriseID, AutoEnabled: boolPtr(true)`
3. Build `installedSet` from both registry IDs

**4F. Modify `InstallMarketplaceServer`** (line 1401)

Set `config.UserId = &claims.Subject` before creating. This makes marketplace installs per-user.

**4G. Personal enterprise behavior**

No special handling needed. Personal enterprises have `personal: true` but the data model is the same. The sole owner of a personal enterprise has full admin rights, so they can manage their own enterprise approved list. The admin role check (`owner || admin`) naturally allows this.

---

### Phase 5: Code Generation

After modifying `openapi.yaml`, run:
```
task generate:api
```

This runs `oapi-codegen` and regenerates `core/api/gen/server.gen.go`, which will:
- Add the `EnterpriseMCPServer` Go type
- Add `UserId` and `Source` fields to `MCPServerConfig`
- Add new methods to `ServerInterface` for the 6 new endpoints
- Add wrapper functions and route registrations

The new `ServerInterface` methods that `apiServer` must implement:
```go
ListEnterpriseMCPServers(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
CreateEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
GetEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
UpdateEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
DeleteEnterpriseMCPServer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
SetEnterpriseMCPServerCredential(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, id string)
```

---

### Phase 6: Test Plan

**6A. New file `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver_test.go`**

Following the pattern of `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`:
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_CRUD` -- create, get, list, update, delete
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_NotFound` -- get/update/delete nonexistent
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_ListWithEnterpriseFilter` -- verify enterprise isolation
- `TestEnterpriseMCPServerStore_ListWithAutoEnabledFilter` -- verify auto_enabled filtering

**6B. Modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`**

- `TestMCPServerStore_ListWithUserFilter` -- create servers for user_a and user_b, verify list with `UserID: "user_a"` returns only user_a's servers

**6C. Handler integration tests** (new file or extend `core/app/handlers_auth_test.go`)

- Test user A cannot see user B's servers
- Test member cannot create/update/delete enterprise MCP servers (gets 403)
- Test admin can CRUD enterprise MCP servers
- Test `ListMCPServers` returns merged personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- Test enterprise auto-enabled servers appear with `source: "enterprise"` and cannot be deleted by user
- Test marketplace `installed` flag reflects both personal and enterprise servers
- Test `InstallMarketplaceServer` creates per-user record
- Test personal enterprise user can manage their own enterprise approved list

---

### Phase 7: UI Changes

**7A. Modify `ui/src/lib/api.ts`**

Add new API functions:
```typescript
export async function listEnterpriseMCPServers() {
    return apiFetch('/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers');
}
export async function createEnterpriseMCPServer(data: any) {
    return apiFetch('/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) });
}
export async function getEnterpriseMCPServer(id: string) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}`);
}
export async function updateEnterpriseMCPServer(id: string, data: any) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}`, { method: 'PUT', body: JSON.stringify(data) });
}
export async function deleteEnterpriseMCPServer(id: string) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' });
}
export async function setEnterpriseMCPServerCredential(id: string, envVarName: string, secretValue: string) {
    return apiFetch(`/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/${id}/credentials`, {
        method: 'POST',
        body: JSON.stringify({ env_var_name: envVarName, secret_value: secretValue })
    });
}
```

**7B. Modify `ui/src/routes/servers/+page.svelte`**

- Display `source` badge on each server card: "Personal" or "Enterprise" (using the `source` field from the API response).
- For enterprise-sourced servers, hide the "Remove" button (or show it disabled with a tooltip "Managed by your organization").
- The existing `listMCPServers()` call already returns the merged list from the backend, so no additional fetch is needed.

**7C. Modify `ui/src/routes/servers/[id]/+page.svelte`**

- Show `source` indicator in the configuration card.
- If `source === "enterprise"`, hide the "Delete Server" danger zone and the credential-setting form (enterprise credentials are managed by admins).

**7D. New route `ui/src/routes/settings/mcp-servers/+page.svelte`**

Enterprise MCP server management page (accessible from settings layout). This is the admin-facing page for curating the approved list:
- Shows enterprise-level MCP servers with `auto_enabled` toggle
- Add/remove servers (calls enterprise endpoints)
- Set credentials for enterprise servers
- Only visible when `getUser()?.role === 'owner' || getUser()?.role === 'admin'`
- For personal enterprises, this is where the user manages their own approved list

**7E. Modify `ui/src/routes/settings/+layout.svelte`**

Add "MCP Servers" navigation link to settings sidebar, visible for owner/admin roles.

**7F. Modify `ui/src/routes/marketplace/+page.svelte`**

The `installed` flag from the API already covers both personal and enterprise sources (handled backend). No changes needed unless we want to distinguish "installed by you" vs "installed by enterprise" -- which could be a follow-up enhancement.

---

### Implementation Sequence

1. **OpenAPI spec** -- all schema and endpoint changes in `core/api/openapi.yaml`
2. **Code generation** -- `task generate:api` to get new types and interface methods
3. **Store SPI** -- update `MCPServerFilter` and add `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` + filter in `core/store/store.go`
4. **In-memory stores** -- modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go` (UserID filter) and create `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go`
5. **Store tests** -- new and modified tests in `core/store/mem/`
6. **Handlers** -- add auth to existing MCP handlers, add enterprise handlers, modify marketplace handlers
7. **Handler tests** -- integration tests for auth, scoping, and merged list behavior
8. **UI api.ts** -- add enterprise MCP API functions
9. **UI pages** -- modify servers pages, add settings/mcp-servers page

### Potential Challenges

- **Backward compatibility**: Existing MCP servers in the store have no `user_id`. The `ListMCPServers` handler should treat `nil` user_id servers as belonging to the requesting user (or as global/legacy) during migration. A pragmatic approach: if `user_id` is nil on a stored record, include it in any user's list. This handles the dev/test scenario where servers were created before auth was wired in.

- **Auth disabled mode**: The existing handlers don't check auth at all. When `claims` is nil (auth middleware skipped), the handlers should fall back to unscoped behavior. Check `if claims != nil { filter.UserID = claims.Subject }` -- when nil, return all servers (backward compatible with local dev).

- **ID prefix collision**: Enterprise MCP servers should use `emcp_` prefix to distinguish from user MCP servers (`mcp_`). This prevents ID collision and makes it clear from the ID alone which store to query.

- **Vault path migration**: Existing vault paths are `mcp-servers/{id}/{env_var}`. New paths will be `mcp-servers/user/{user_id}/{id}/{env_var}` for user servers and `mcp-servers/enterprise/{enterprise_id}/{id}/{env_var}` for enterprise servers. Existing paths should continue to work for legacy servers.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/openapi.yaml`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/store.go`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app/handlers.go`
- `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/app/handlers_auth.go`
Now let me write the final plan.

input.content:
# Per-User + Enterprise-Governed MCP Server Management

## Context

MCP server installations are currently global — all users share the same server list. This change introduces two-layer scoping:

1. **Per-user**: Each user has their own installed servers (invisible to other users)
2. **Enterprise-governed**: Enterprise admins curate an "approved list" and can auto-enable servers for all users (mandatory, non-removable by members)

Personal accounts (Gmail etc.) are their own enterprise owners and manage their own approved list.

---

## Data Model

### User-level: `MCPServerConfig` (existing, modified)
- Add `user_id` (readOnly string) — the owning user
- Add `source` (readOnly enum: `personal` | `enterprise`) — distinguishes origin in merged list responses

### Enterprise-level: `EnterpriseMCPServer` (new)
- `id` (readOnly, `emcp_` prefix)
- `enterprise_id` (readOnly)
- Same server config fields: name, description, command, env, mode, version, registry_id, policy_mapping
- `auto_enabled` (boolean, default false) — if true, mandatory for all enterprise users
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (readOnly)

### User's effective server list
`GET /v1/mcp-servers` returns the merge of:
- Personal servers (`user_id` = current user, `source: "personal"`)
- Enterprise auto-enabled servers (`enterprise_id` = user's enterprise, `auto_enabled: true`, `source: "enterprise"`)

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: OpenAPI Spec (`core/api/openapi.yaml`)

**Schema changes:**
- Add `user_id` and `source` fields to `MCPServerConfig`
- Add new `EnterpriseMCPServer` schema
- Add 401/403 error responses to all MCP server endpoints

**New endpoints (tag: `Enterprise MCP Servers`):**

| Method | Path | operationId | Auth |
|--------|------|-------------|------|
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | listEnterpriseMCPServers | Any authenticated |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | createEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | getEnterpriseMCPServer | Any authenticated |
| PUT | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | updateEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| DELETE | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | deleteEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}/credentials` | setEnterpriseMCPServerCredential | owner/admin |

### Step 2: Code Generation

```sh
task generate:api
```

### Step 3: Store SPI (`core/store/store.go`)

- Add `UserID string` to `MCPServerFilter`
- Add new `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` interface (Create/Get/List/Update/Delete)
- Add `EnterpriseMCPServerFilter` with `EnterpriseID string`, `AutoEnabled *bool`, `PageSize int`

### Step 4: In-Memory Stores

**Modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`:**
- `List()`: filter by `UserID` when set

**New file `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go`:**
- Same pattern as mcpserver.go
- Filter by `EnterpriseID` and optionally `AutoEnabled`

### Step 5: Handlers (`core/app/handlers.go`)

**Auth enforcement on ALL existing MCP handlers:**
- Extract claims; return 401 if nil (when auth is enabled)
- Graceful fallback: if claims are nil (auth disabled), skip user scoping for local dev

**Modified handlers:**
- `ListMCPServers` — filter by `UserID`, merge enterprise auto-enabled servers with `source` field
- `CreateMCPServer` — set `user_id` from claims
- `GetMCPServer` — verify ownership or enterprise auto-enabled membership
- `UpdateMCPServer` — verify ownership; 403 if enterprise-sourced
- `DeleteMCPServer` — verify ownership; 403 if enterprise-sourced
- `SetMCPServerCredential` — verify ownership
- `ListMarketplaceServers` — `installed` reflects personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- `InstallMarketplaceServer` — set `user_id` from claims

**New enterprise handlers** (in handlers.go or new file):
- `ListEnterpriseMCPServers` — filter by `claims.EnterpriseID`
- `CreateEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check, set `enterprise_id`, `emcp_` prefix ID
- `GetEnterpriseMCPServer` — verify enterprise match
- `UpdateEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check, preserve read-only fields
- `DeleteEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check
- `SetEnterpriseMCPServerCredential` — admin check, vault path: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{ent_id}/{id}/{env_var}`

### Step 6: Tests

**`core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`:**
- Add: two users create servers, List with UserID returns only that user's servers

**New `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver_test.go`:**
- CRUD tests
- Enterprise isolation (list by enterprise_id)
- Auto-enabled filtering

**Handler/integration tests:**
- User A cannot see user B's servers
- Member gets 403 on enterprise CRUD writes
- Admin can CRUD enterprise servers
- ListMCPServers returns merged personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- Enterprise auto-enabled servers show `source: "enterprise"` and cannot be deleted by member
- Marketplace `installed` reflects both sources

### Step 7: UI Changes

**`ui/src/lib/api.ts`:** Add enterprise MCP server API functions

**`ui/src/routes/servers/+page.svelte`:**
- Show `source` badge (Personal / Enterprise) on each server card
- Hide delete button for enterprise-sourced servers

**`ui/src/routes/servers/[id]/+page.svelte`:**
- Show source indicator
- Hide delete/credential forms for enterprise-sourced servers

**New `ui/src/routes/settings/mcp-servers/+page.svelte`:**
- Admin page to manage enterprise approved list
- Add/remove servers, toggle `auto_enabled`, set credentials
- Visible for owner/admin roles only

**`ui/src/routes/marketplace/+page.svelte`:** No changes needed (backend handles `installed` scoping)

---

## Design Decisions

- **`emcp_` prefix** for enterprise MCP server IDs vs `mcp_` for user IDs — prevents collision and makes store routing clear
- **Auth disabled fallback**: When claims are nil, MCP handlers skip user scoping (backward compat for local dev without auth)
- **Vault path namespacing**: User servers: `mcp-servers/{id}/{env_var}` (unchanged for compatibility). Enterprise servers: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{ent_id}/{id}/{env_var}`
- **No migration needed**: In-memory store is ephemeral. Nil `user_id` on legacy records included in any user's list as fallback

---

# Per-User + Enterprise-Governed MCP Server Management

## Context

MCP server installations are currently global — all users share the same server list. This change introduces two-layer scoping:

1. **Per-user**: Each user has their own installed servers (invisible to other users)
2. **Enterprise-governed**: Enterprise admins curate an "approved list" and can auto-enable servers for all users (mandatory, non-removable by members)

Personal accounts (Gmail etc.) are their own enterprise owners and manage their own approved list.

---

## Data Model

### User-level: `MCPServerConfig` (existing, modified)
- Add `user_id` (readOnly string) — the owning user
- Add `source` (readOnly enum: `personal` | `enterprise`) — distinguishes origin in merged list responses

### Enterprise-level: `EnterpriseMCPServer` (new)
- `id` (readOnly, `emcp_` prefix)
- `enterprise_id` (readOnly)
- Same server config fields: name, description, command, env, mode, version, registry_id, policy_mapping
- `auto_enabled` (boolean, default false) — if true, mandatory for all enterprise users
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (readOnly)

### User's effective server list
`GET /v1/mcp-servers` returns the merge of:
- Personal servers (`user_id` = current user, `source: "personal"`)
- Enterprise auto-enabled servers (`enterprise_id` = user's enterprise, `auto_enabled: true`, `source: "enterprise"`)

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: OpenAPI Spec (`core/api/openapi.yaml`)

**Schema changes:**
- Add `user_id` and `source` fields to `MCPServerConfig`
- Add new `EnterpriseMCPServer` schema
- Add 401/403 error responses to all MCP server endpoints

**New endpoints (tag: `Enterprise MCP Servers`):**

| Method | Path | operationId | Auth |
|--------|------|-------------|------|
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | listEnterpriseMCPServers | Any authenticated |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | createEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | getEnterpriseMCPServer | Any authenticated |
| PUT | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | updateEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| DELETE | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | deleteEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}/credentials` | setEnterpriseMCPServerCredential | owner/admin |

### Step 2: Code Generation

```sh
task generate:api
```

### Step 3: Store SPI (`core/store/store.go`)

- Add `UserID string` to `MCPServerFilter`
- Add new `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` interface (Create/Get/List/Update/Delete)
- Add `EnterpriseMCPServerFilter` with `EnterpriseID string`, `AutoEnabled *bool`, `PageSize int`

### Step 4: In-Memory Stores

**Modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`:**
- `List()`: filter by `UserID` when set

**New file `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go`:**
- Same pattern as mcpserver.go
- Filter by `EnterpriseID` and optionally `AutoEnabled`

### Step 5: Handlers (`core/app/handlers.go`)

**Auth enforcement on ALL existing MCP handlers:**
- Extract claims; return 401 if nil (when auth is enabled)
- Graceful fallback: if claims are nil (auth disabled), skip user scoping for local dev

**Modified handlers:**
- `ListMCPServers` — filter by `UserID`, merge enterprise auto-enabled servers with `source` field
- `CreateMCPServer` — set `user_id` from claims
- `GetMCPServer` — verify ownership or enterprise auto-enabled membership
- `UpdateMCPServer` — verify ownership; 403 if enterprise-sourced
- `DeleteMCPServer` — verify ownership; 403 if enterprise-sourced
- `SetMCPServerCredential` — verify ownership
- `ListMarketplaceServers` — `installed` reflects personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- `InstallMarketplaceServer` — set `user_id` from claims

**New enterprise handlers** (in handlers.go or new file):
- `ListEnterpriseMCPServers` — filter by `claims.EnterpriseID`
- `CreateEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check, set `enterprise_id`, `emcp_` prefix ID
- `GetEnterpriseMCPServer` — verify enterprise match
- `UpdateEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check, preserve read-only fields
- `DeleteEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check
- `SetEnterpriseMCPServerCredential` — admin check, vault path: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{ent_id}/{id}/{env_var}`

### Step 6: Tests

**`core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`:**
- Add: two users create servers, List with UserID returns only that user's servers

**New `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver_test.go`:**
- CRUD tests
- Enterprise isolation (list by enterprise_id)
- Auto-enabled filtering

**Handler/integration tests:**
- User A cannot see user B's servers
- Member gets 403 on enterprise CRUD writes
- Admin can CRUD enterprise servers
- ListMCPServers returns merged personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- Enterprise auto-enabled servers show `source: "enterprise"` and cannot be deleted by member
- Marketplace `installed` reflects both sources

### Step 7: UI Changes

**`ui/src/lib/api.ts`:** Add enterprise MCP server API functions

**`ui/src/routes/servers/+page.svelte`:**
- Show `source` badge (Personal / Enterprise) on each server card
- Hide delete button for enterprise-sourced servers

**`ui/src/routes/servers/[id]/+page.svelte`:**
- Show source indicator
- Hide delete/credential forms for enterprise-sourced servers

**New `ui/src/routes/settings/mcp-servers/+page.svelte`:**
- Admin page to manage enterprise approved list
- Add/remove servers, toggle `auto_enabled`, set credentials
- Visible for owner/admin roles only

**`ui/src/routes/marketplace/+page.svelte`:** No changes needed (backend handles `installed` scoping)

---

## Design Decisions

- **`emcp_` prefix** for enterprise MCP server IDs vs `mcp_` for user IDs — prevents collision and makes store routing clear
- **Auth disabled fallback**: When claims are nil, MCP handlers skip user scoping (backward compat for local dev without auth)
- **Vault path namespacing**: User servers: `mcp-servers/{id}/{env_var}` (unchanged for compatibility). Enterprise servers: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{ent_id}/{id}/{env_var}`
- **No migration needed**: In-memory store is ephemeral. Nil `user_id` on legacy records included in any user's list as fallback

---

## Files to Modify/Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `core/api/openapi.yaml` | Modify: add schemas, endpoints, error responses |
| `core/api/gen/server.gen.go` | Regenerated |
| `core/store/store.go` | Modify: add UserID to filter, new EnterpriseMCPServerStore |
| `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go` | Modify: UserID filtering in List |
| `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go` | **New**: enterprise store impl |
| `core/app/handlers.go` | Modify: auth + user scoping on all MCP handlers |
| `core/app/handlers.go` (or new file) | Add: enterprise MCP handlers |
| `core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go` | Modify: add user-scoped tests |
| `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver_test.go` | **New**: enterprise store tests |
| `ui/src/lib/api.ts` | Modify: add enterprise API functions |
| `ui/src/routes/servers/+page.svelte` | Modify: source badge, conditional delete |
| `ui/src/routes/servers/[id]/+page.svelte` | Modify: source indicator, conditional forms |
| `ui/src/routes/settings/mcp-servers/+page.svelte` | **New**: enterprise MCP admin page |

## Verification

1. `task generate:api` succeeds
2. `go build ./...` compiles
3. `go test ./core/store/mem/...` — all store tests pass
4. `go test ./core/app/...` — all handler tests pass
5. `go test ./...` — full suite passes
6. Manual: auth as user A, install server, auth as user B, verify B's list is empty
7. Manual: auth as admin, add enterprise auto-enabled server, verify it appears for member
8. Manual: verify member cannot delete enterprise auto-enabled server

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Per-User MCP Server Scoping

## Context

MCP server installations are currently global — all users share the same server list. When user A installs a server, user B sees it too. This change scopes all MCP server CRUD and marketplace operations to the authenticated user via `user_id`, so each user has their own independent set of installed servers.

## Approach

Thread user identity (from JWT claims) through MCP endpoints, store, and filters. The scoping is entirely server-side; no UI changes are needed.

## Steps

### 1. OpenAPI Spec (`core/api/openapi.yaml`)

- Add `user_id` (readOnly string) to `MCPServerConfig` schema
- Add `UserID` to `MCPServerFilter` (implicit — not in OpenAPI, in Go SPI)
- Add `401`/`403` error responses to all MCP server endpoints and marketplace endpoints
- Run `task generate:api` to regenerate `core/api/gen/server.gen.go`

### 2. Store SPI (`core/store/store.go`)

- Add `UserID string` field to `MCPServerFilter`

### 3. In-Memory Store (`core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`)

- In `List()`: if `filter.UserID != ""`, skip servers where `UserId` doesn't match
- In `Get()`: no change (ownership enforced at handler level)

### 4. Handlers (`core/app/handlers.go`)

Add a helper to extract user ID from claims:
```go
func userIDFromRequest(r *http.Request) string {
    if c := auth.ClaimsFromContext(r.Context()); c != nil {
        return c.Subject
    }
    return ""
}
```

**Each MCP handler** gains auth enforcement:
- Extract `userID` via helper; return 401 if empty
- **ListMCPServers**: pass `UserID` in filter
- **CreateMCPServer**: set `UserId` on the config before storing
- **GetMCPServer**: after fetching, verify `UserId` matches; return 404 if not
- **UpdateMCPServer**: same ownership check as Get
- **DeleteMCPServer**: same ownership check
- **SetMCPServerCredential**: same ownership check
- **ListMarketplaceServers**: scope the "installed" set to the current user
- **InstallMarketplaceServer**: set `UserId` on the created config

### 5. Tests (`core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`)

- Add test: two users create servers, List with UserID filter returns only that user's servers
- Add test: List without UserID filter returns all (for admin/system use)
- Verify existing CRUD tests still pass

### 6. UI — No Changes

API handles scoping server-side. The UI already sends auth tokens. The `user_id` field will appear in responses but the UI doesn't need to use it.

## Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `core/api/openapi.yaml` | Add `user_id` field, auth error responses |
| `core/api/gen/server.gen.go` | Regenerated (never hand-edit) |
| `core/store/store.go` | Add `UserID` to `MCPServerFilter` |
| `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go` | Filter by `UserID` in `List()` |
| `core/app/handlers.go` | Auth enforcement + user scoping in all MCP handlers |
| `core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go` | Add user-scoped filtering tests |

## Verification

1. `task generate:api` succeeds
2. `go build ./...` compiles
3. `go test ./core/store/mem/...` — store tests pass (including new user-scoped tests)
4. `go test ./core/app/...` — existing handler tests pass
5. `go test ./...` — full test suite passes
6. Manual: start server, authenticate as user A, install a server, authenticate as user B, verify B's server list is empty

allowedPrompts.prompt: run task generate:api to regenerate OpenAPI code
run go build to verify compilation
run go test to verify tests pass
run npm/pnpm commands for UI build

- Same server config fields: name, description, command, env, mode, version, registry_id, policy_mapping
- `auto_enabled` (boolean, default false) — if true, mandatory for all enterprise users
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (readOnly)

### User's effective server list
`GET /v1/mcp-servers` returns the merge of:
- Personal servers (`user_id` = current user, `source: "personal"`)
- Enterprise auto-enabled servers (`enterprise_id` = user's enterprise, `auto_enabled: true`, `source: "enterprise"`)

---

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: OpenAPI Spec (`core/api/openapi.yaml`)

**Schema changes:**
- Add `user_id` and `source` fields to `MCPServerConfig`
- Add new `EnterpriseMCPServer` schema
- Add 401/403 error responses to all MCP server endpoints

**New endpoints (tag: `Enterprise MCP Servers`):**

| Method | Path | operationId | Auth |
|--------|------|-------------|------|
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | listEnterpriseMCPServers | Any authenticated |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers` | createEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| GET | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | getEnterpriseMCPServer | Any authenticated |
| PUT | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | updateEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| DELETE | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}` | deleteEnterpriseMCPServer | owner/admin |
| POST | `/v1/enterprise/mcp-servers/{id}/credentials` | setEnterpriseMCPServerCredential | owner/admin |

### Step 2: Code Generation

```sh
task generate:api
```

### Step 3: Store SPI (`core/store/store.go`)

- Add `UserID string` to `MCPServerFilter`
- Add new `EnterpriseMCPServerStore` interface (Create/Get/List/Update/Delete)
- Add `EnterpriseMCPServerFilter` with `EnterpriseID string`, `AutoEnabled *bool`, `PageSize int`

### Step 4: In-Memory Stores

**Modify `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go`:**
- `List()`: filter by `UserID` when set

**New file `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go`:**
- Same pattern as mcpserver.go
- Filter by `EnterpriseID` and optionally `AutoEnabled`

### Step 5: Handlers (`core/app/handlers.go`)

**Auth enforcement on ALL existing MCP handlers:**
- Extract claims; return 401 if nil (when auth is enabled)
- Graceful fallback: if claims are nil (auth disabled), skip user scoping for local dev

**Modified handlers:**
- `ListMCPServers` — filter by `UserID`, merge enterprise auto-enabled servers with `source` field
- `CreateMCPServer` — set `user_id` from claims
- `GetMCPServer` — verify ownership or enterprise auto-enabled membership
- `UpdateMCPServer` — verify ownership; 403 if enterprise-sourced
- `DeleteMCPServer` — verify ownership; 403 if enterprise-sourced
- `SetMCPServerCredential` — verify ownership
- `ListMarketplaceServers` — `installed` reflects personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- `InstallMarketplaceServer` — set `user_id` from claims

**New enterprise handlers** (in handlers.go or new file):
- `ListEnterpriseMCPServers` — filter by `claims.EnterpriseID`
- `CreateEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check, set `enterprise_id`, `emcp_` prefix ID
- `GetEnterpriseMCPServer` — verify enterprise match
- `UpdateEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check, preserve read-only fields
- `DeleteEnterpriseMCPServer` — admin check
- `SetEnterpriseMCPServerCredential` — admin check, vault path: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{ent_id}/{id}/{env_var}`

### Step 6: Tests

**`core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go`:**
- Add: two users create servers, List with UserID returns only that user's servers

**New `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver_test.go`:**
- CRUD tests
- Enterprise isolation (list by enterprise_id)
- Auto-enabled filtering

**Handler/integration tests:**
- User A cannot see user B's servers
- Member gets 403 on enterprise CRUD writes
- Admin can CRUD enterprise servers
- ListMCPServers returns merged personal + enterprise auto-enabled
- Enterprise auto-enabled servers show `source: "enterprise"` and cannot be deleted by member
- Marketplace `installed` reflects both sources

### Step 7: UI Changes

**`ui/src/lib/api.ts`:** Add enterprise MCP server API functions

**`ui/src/routes/servers/+page.svelte`:**
- Show `source` badge (Personal / Enterprise) on each server card
- Hide delete button for enterprise-sourced servers

**`ui/src/routes/servers/[id]/+page.svelte`:**
- Show source indicator
- Hide delete/credential forms for enterprise-sourced servers

**New `ui/src/routes/settings/mcp-servers/+page.svelte`:**
- Admin page to manage enterprise approved list
- Add/remove servers, toggle `auto_enabled`, set credentials
- Visible for owner/admin roles only

**`ui/src/routes/marketplace/+page.svelte`:** No changes needed (backend handles `installed` scoping)

---

## Design Decisions

- **`emcp_` prefix** for enterprise MCP server IDs vs `mcp_` for user IDs — prevents collision and makes store routing clear
- **Auth disabled fallback**: When claims are nil, MCP handlers skip user scoping (backward compat for local dev without auth)
- **Vault path namespacing**: User servers: `mcp-servers/{id}/{env_var}` (unchanged for compatibility). Enterprise servers: `mcp-servers/enterprise/{ent_id}/{id}/{env_var}`
- **No migration needed**: In-memory store is ephemeral. Nil `user_id` on legacy records included in any user's list as fallback

---

## Files to Modify/Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `core/api/openapi.yaml` | Modify: add schemas, endpoints, error responses |
| `core/api/gen/server.gen.go` | Regenerated |
| `core/store/store.go` | Modify: add UserID to filter, new EnterpriseMCPServerStore |
| `core/store/mem/mcpserver.go` | Modify: UserID filtering in List |
| `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver.go` | **New**: enterprise store impl |
| `core/app/handlers.go` | Modify: auth + user scoping on all MCP handlers |
| `core/app/handlers.go` (or new file) | Add: enterprise MCP handlers |
| `core/store/mem/mcpserver_test.go` | Modify: add user-scoped tests |
| `core/store/mem/enterprise_mcpserver_test.go` | **New**: enterprise store tests |
| `ui/src/lib/api.ts` | Modify: add enterprise API functions |
| `ui/src/routes/servers/+page.svelte` | Modify: source badge, conditional delete |
| `ui/src/routes/servers/[id]/+page.svelte` | Modify: source indicator, conditional forms |
| `ui/src/routes/settings/mcp-servers/+page.svelte` | **New**: enterprise MCP admin page |

## Verification

1. `task generate:api` succeeds
2. `go build ./...` compiles
3. `go test ./core/store/mem/...` — all store tests pass
4. `go test ./core/app/...` — all handler tests pass
5. `go test ./...` — full suite passes
6. Manual: auth as user A, install server, auth as user B, verify B's list is empty
7. Manual: auth as admin, add enterprise auto-enabled server, verify it appears for member
8. Manual: verify member cannot delete enterprise auto-enabled server

toolUseResult.plan:
# Per-User + Enterprise-Governed MCP Server Management

## Context

MCP server installations are currently global — all users share the same server list. This change introduces two-layer scoping:

1. **Per-user**: Each user has their own installed servers (invisible to other users)
2. **Enterprise-governed**: Enterprise admins curate an "approved list" and can auto-enable servers for all users (mandatory, non-removable by members)

Personal accounts (Gmail etc.) are their own enterprise owners and manage their own approved list.

---

## Data Model

### User-level: `MCPServerConfig` (existing, modified)
- Add `user_id` (readOnly string) — the owning user
- Add `source` (readOnly enum: `personal` | `enterprise`) — distinguishes origin in merged list responses

### Enterprise-level: `EnterpriseMCPServer` (new)
- `id` (readOnly, `emcp_` prefix)
- `enterprise_id` (readOnly)
- Same server config fields: name, description, command, env, mode, version, registry_id, policy_mapping
- `auto_enabled` (boolean, default false) — if true, mandatory for all enterprise users
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (readOnly)

### User's effective server list
`GET /v1/mcp-servers` returns the merge of:
- Personal servers (`user_id` = current user, `source: "personal"`)
- Enterprise auto-enabled servers (`enterprise_id` = user's enterprise, `auto_enabled: true`, `source: "enterprise"`)

---

openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Agentic Control Plane API
  version: 0.2.0
  summary: Policy, approval, execution, and audit APIs for governed agent actions
  description: |
    v0.2 extends the base control-plane lifecycle with richer, domain-specific schemas for:
      - Git and pull request actions
      - Deploy and cloud actions
      - Email and calendar actions
      - Payment and procurement actions

    Core lifecycle:
      intent -> policy decision -> approval (optional) -> execution grant -> execution -> trace

servers:
  - url: https://api.withaileron.ai
    description: Production

security:
  - bearerAuth: []

tags:
  - name: Auth
  - name: Enterprises
  - name: Users
  - name: Intents
  - name: Approvals
  - name: Policies
  - name: Connectors
  - name: Executions
  - name: Funding Sources
  - name: Credentials
  - name: Traces
  - name: Analytics
  - name: MCP Servers
  - name: Health

paths:
  /v1/health:
    get:
      tags: [Health]
      summary: Get service health
      operationId: getHealth
      security: []
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Service health
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/HealthResponse"

  /auth/{provider}/login:
    get:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Initiate OAuth login
      description: Redirects the user to the specified auth provider's consent screen.
      operationId: authLogin
      security: []
      parameters:
        - name: provider
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            examples: [google, github]
      responses:
        "307":
          description: Redirect to auth provider
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"

  /auth/{provider}/callback:
    get:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: OAuth callback
      description: |
        Handles the OAuth callback from the auth provider. Exchanges the
        authorization code for user identity, creates or looks up the user
        and enterprise, issues session tokens, and redirects to the UI.
      operationId: authCallback
      security: []
      parameters:
        - name: provider
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: code
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
        - name: state
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        "307":
          description: Redirect to UI with session cookies set
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
        "403":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Forbidden"

  /auth/signup:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Sign up with email and password
      description: |
        Creates a new account with email and password. The account starts in
        pending_verification status. A 6-digit verification code is sent to
        the email address and must be submitted via /auth/verify-email before
        the account can be used.
      operationId: authSignup
      security: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/SignupRequest"
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Account created, verification code sent
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/SignupResponse"
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "409":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Conflict"

  /auth/verify-email:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Verify email address
      description: |
        Submits the 6-digit verification code sent to the user's email.
        On success, the account status changes from pending_verification to active.
      operationId: authVerifyEmail
      security: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/VerifyEmailRequest"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Email verified, account activated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/VerifyEmailResponse"
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "404":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/NotFound"
        "409":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Conflict"

  /auth/login:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Log in with email and password
      description: Authenticates with email and password and returns an access token.
      operationId: authEmailLogin
      security: []
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/EmailLoginRequest"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Authenticated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/RefreshTokenResponse"
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
        "403":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Forbidden"

  /auth/refresh:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Refresh access token
      description: Exchanges a refresh token for a new access token. The refresh token is rotated.
      operationId: authRefresh
      security: []
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/RefreshTokenRequest"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: New access token
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/RefreshTokenResponse"
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"

  /auth/logout:
    post:
      tags: [Auth]
      summary: Log out
      description: Deletes the session and clears authentication cookies.
      operationId: authLogout
      security: []
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Logged out
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LogoutResponse"

  /v1/enterprises/me:
    get:
      tags: [Enterprises]
      summary: Get current enterprise
      description: Returns the enterprise for the authenticated user.
      operationId: getCurrentEnterprise
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Current enterprise
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/Enterprise"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"

    patch:
      tags: [Enterprises]
      summary: Update current enterprise
      description: Updates the authenticated user's enterprise. Requires owner or admin role.
      operationId: updateCurrentEnterprise
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/UpdateEnterpriseRequest"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Enterprise updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/Enterprise"
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
        "403":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Forbidden"

  /v1/users/me:
    get:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Get current user
      description: Returns the authenticated user's profile.
      operationId: getCurrentUser
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Current user
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/User"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"

    patch:
      tags: [Users]
      summary: Update current user
      description: Updates the authenticated user's profile.
      operationId: updateCurrentUser
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/UpdateUserRequest"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: User updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/User"
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"

  /v1/intents:
    post:
      tags: [Intents]
      summary: Submit a new action intent
      operationId: createIntent
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/CreateIntentRequest"
            examples:
              gitPullRequest:
                summary: Create a pull request
                value:
                  idempotency_key: "REDACTED"
                  agent_id: "agt_codex_repo_alpha"
                  workspace_id: "ws_engineering"
                  action:
                    type: "git.pull_request.create"
                    summary: "Open PR from feature branch to main"
                    justification: "Ship checkout bug fix"
                    target:
                      kind: "repository"
                      id: "repo_checkout"
                      display_name: "github.com/acme/checkout"
                    domain:
                      git:
                        provider: "github"
                        repository: "acme/checkout"
                        branch: "fix/checkout-tax-rounding"
                        base_branch: "main"
                        commit_shas:
                          - "abc1234"
                        files_changed:
                          - "src/tax.ts"
                          - "tests/tax.test.ts"
                        pr_title: "Fix tax rounding on checkout"
                        pr_body: "This PR fixes checkout tax rounding and adds regression tests."
                        labels: ["bugfix", "checkout"]
                  context:
                    environment: "production"
                    source_platform: "codex"
              paymentPurchase:
                summary: Purchase SaaS subscription
                value:
                  idempotency_key: "REDACTED"
                  agent_id: "agt_procurement_ops"
                  workspace_id: "ws_finops"
                  action:
                    type: "payment.charge"
                    summary: "Purchase annual Figma license"
                    justification: "Renew expiring seat for jane@company.com"
                    target:
                      kind: "vendor"
                      id: "vendor_figma"
                      display_name: "Figma"
                    domain:
                      payment:
                        vendor_name: "Figma"
                        amount:
                          amount: 18000
                          currency: "USD"
                        merchant_category: "software"
                        budget_code: "design-software"
                        funding_source_id: "fs_team_software"
                        payment_instrument_preference: "virtual_card"
                        line_items:
                          - description: "Figma Pro Annual"
                            quantity: 1
                            unit_amount:
                              amount: 18000
                              currency: "USD"
                        beneficiary:
                          email: "jane@company.com"
                  context:
                    environment: "production"
                    source_platform: "openclaw"
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Intent created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentEnvelope"
        "400":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/BadRequest"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
        "403":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Forbidden"
        "409":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Conflict"

    get:
      tags: [Intents]
      summary: List intents
      operationId: listIntents
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/PageSize"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/PageToken"
        - name: workspace_id
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string

        - name: action_type
          in: query

            type: string
        - name: agent_id
          in: query

      responses:
        "200":
          description: Paginated intents
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/IntentListResponse"
        "401":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Unauthorized"
        "403":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Forbidden"

  /v1/intents/{intent_id}:

        "403":

        "404":

  /v1/intents/{intent_id}/evidence:

        "400":

        "403":

        "404":

  /v1/approvals:
